source.section: 6 - Markpoints > 6.2 - How to Add Markpoints > Overfly Method

steps.action: Make sure that no target is designated with any sensor (targeting pod, air-to-ground radar, waypoint designate). Use the Undesignate Button if you wish to un-designate a target.
steps.voiced: Confirm no target is designated, then press Undesignate Button to clear.

steps.action: Fly over the location you want to mark
steps.voiced: Fly directly over the location you want to mark.

steps.action: From either the HSI (Horizontal Situation Indicator) or the SA (Situational Awareness) page, press the OSB (Option Select Button) next to MK#.
steps.voiced: On the HSI or SA page, press the OSB next to MK#.

steps.action: The MK# will increment and the markpoint will be created at the coordinates you were at when pressing the MK# OSB. (Note: Once MK#9 is created (the last of the 9 available markpoints), the next created MK# will overwrite MK#1, and so on and so forth.) 6 - MARKPOINTS
steps.voiced: MK# increments and the markpoint is set at your position — after MK#9, it wraps back to MK#1.
